findings.message: Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. The command cannot be registered as a slash command in the Claude Code plugin index without a name. The plugin loader silently skips nameless commands on install.
No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. This issue compounds the missing `name` bug — the command is unregisterable AND underdeclared.
No empty-input handling. The audit-context command requires a workspace or item scope to be useful. Without an argument guard it will run a generic audit with no target.

Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. Same effect as audit-context.md — this migration command cannot be registered or invoked. Both commands in the fabric-cli plugin are currently dead on install.
No `model:` frontmatter field. This multi-step migration command involves irreversible capacity operations; the runtime should route it to a capable model explicitly rather than inheriting a default.
No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Compounds the missing `name` and missing `model` bugs — the command is triple-underdeclared.

No empty-input handling. A capacity migration command that runs without a target argument could attempt to operate against an unintended scope. Empty-argument detection is especially important for irreversible operations.
Agent has no explicit `## Output Format` section. The process steps describe what the agent produces implicitly, but consumers cannot rely on a stable output contract without a dedicated section.
Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. Three of the four reviewer agents (r-reviewer, svg-reviewer, python-reviewer) share this gap; deneb-reviewer is the exception.

Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples to illustrate distinct input contexts. The single example covers a basic R visual review but leaves more complex or edge-case scenarios undocumented.
Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. The deneb-reviewer agent in the same plugin has 2 examples; this agent is inconsistently documented within the custom-visuals/reports plugin.
No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Commands should declare which tools they may invoke so the runtime can enforce least-privilege constraints and users can audit the command's capabilities.

No empty-input handling. If a user invokes `/suggest-rule` with no argument, the command will proceed without a rule description, producing a confusing or empty output. The command should detect empty `$ARGUMENTS` and ask for a description.
Vague quantifier: "appropriate Severity" (×2). The command leaves severity assignment to judgment without specifying the mapping. This leads to inconsistent rule severity across teams.

Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. The command cannot be registered as a slash command in the Claude Code plugin index without a name. The plugin loader silently skips nameless commands on install. — ---
description: ...

Frontmatter missing required `name:` field. Same effect as audit-context.md — this migration command cannot be registered or invoked. Both commands in the fabric-cli plugin are currently dead on install. — ---
description: ...

Agent has no explicit `## Output Format` section. The process steps describe what the agent produces implicitly, but consumers cannot rely on a stable output contract without a dedicated section. — Process steps describe output implicitly within numbered steps but no `## Output Format` heading exists

Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples to illustrate distinct input contexts. The single example covers a basic R visual review but leaves more complex or edge-case scenarios undocumented. — Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition

Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. The deneb-reviewer agent in the same plugin has 2 examples; this agent is inconsistently documented within the custom-visuals/reports plugin. — Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition

Only 1 example block. Convention requires ≥2 examples. Three of the four reviewer agents (r-reviewer, svg-reviewer, python-reviewer) share this gap; deneb-reviewer is the exception. — Only one `## Example` block present in the agent definition

No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Commands should declare which tools they may invoke so the runtime can enforce least-privilege constraints and users can audit the command's capabilities. — Frontmatter has `name`, `description`, `argument-hint`, `model` but no `allowed-tools` field

No empty-input handling. If a user invokes `/suggest-rule` with no argument, the command will proceed without a rule description, producing a confusing or empty output. The command should detect empty `$ARGUMENTS` and ask for a description. — Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` for rule description but provides no guard or prompt when invoked without arguments

Vague quantifier: "appropriate Severity" (×2). The command leaves severity assignment to judgment without specifying the mapping. This leads to inconsistent rule severity across teams. — "appropriate Severity" appears twice in the rule generation workflow

No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. This issue compounds the missing `name` bug — the command is unregisterable AND underdeclared. — Frontmatter has `description`, `argument-hint`, `model` but no `allowed-tools` field

No empty-input handling. The audit-context command requires a workspace or item scope to be useful. Without an argument guard it will run a generic audit with no target. — Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` but provides no guard when invoked without a workspace or scope argument

No `model:` frontmatter field. This multi-step migration command involves irreversible capacity operations; the runtime should route it to a capable model explicitly rather than inheriting a default. — Frontmatter has `description` and `argument-hint` but no `model:` field

No `allowed-tools:` frontmatter field. Compounds the missing `name` and missing `model` bugs — the command is triple-underdeclared. — Frontmatter has `description`, `argument-hint` but no `allowed-tools` field

No empty-input handling. A capacity migration command that runs without a target argument could attempt to operate against an unintended scope. Empty-argument detection is especially important for irreversible operations. — Command uses `$ARGUMENTS` for capacity/workspace target but provides no guard for empty invocation
